items.title: Keep .md extension in displayed file names
items.body: Do not trim the .md suffix when rendering file names in the UI.

items.title: Home page: remove first section and add search to second
items.body: Remove the first section on the home page and add search functionality to the second section.

items.title: Render files as a tree using shadcn tree control
items.body: Show files in a tree view using a built-in shadcn component/control.

items.title: Settings: configurable context/comment prefixes for copy
items.body: Add settings options to change the prefix used for context and comment when copy is clicked.

items.title: Prepend file path when copying content
items.body: When copy runs, prepend the file path at the beginning to improve context for AI agents.

items.title: Investigate how file comments/data are persisted
items.body: Document and verify where and how comment/data are persisted per file.

items.title: Settings: configurable comments delimiter
items.body: Add a settings option to configure the delimiter used between comments when copying multiple comments.

items.title: Settings: file path mode for copy (relative/full)
items.body: Add an option to include file path as relative or full path in copied output.

items.title: Add hideable document outline panel
items.body: Create a left-side panel that shows the document outline similar to MS Word. The panel should be hideable so users can toggle it on and off while editing or reading.

items.title: Add Mermaid graph rendering with raw-text comments only
items.body: Render Mermaid diagrams in markdown content. Ensure comments can only be attached to the raw Mermaid source text, not directly to the rendered graph output.

items.title: Add image preview feature
items.body: Add image preview support so users can view images more clearly from the document experience.

items.title: Enable direct document editing
items.body: Allow users to edit the document content directly within the application instead of only viewing it.

items.title: [SPA-API] SPA-API-18A Remove legacy SSR fallback handlers
items.body: Audit and remove legacy React Router SSR fallback behavior and transitional server-rendered route fallbacks. Verify production runtime no longer depends on SSR fallback handlers once SPA deep-link fallback is in place.

items.title: [LEXICAL] Migrate viewer-only markdown flow to a Lexical editor
items.body: Replace the current view-only markdown experience with a Lexical-based editor that loads local .md files, preserves markdown-compatible content, saves changes back to disk, and supports images through a file-backed workflow.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-01 Inventory markdown round-trip requirements
items.body: Document which existing markdown constructs must survive import/export without loss, including headings, lists, tables, code fences, links, images, Mermaid blocks, and annotation-adjacent behavior. Define what stays read-only or falls back to raw markdown when Lexical cannot round-trip safely.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-03 Add server markdown write service
items.body: Extend the server file layer with a write service that saves markdown content back to disk under the configured content root.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-05 Add frontend save client and dirty-state tracking
items.body: Add API client support for saving edited markdown and track dirty, saving, and error states in the editor UI.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-02 Select Lexical nodes and plugins for markdown-safe editing
items.body: Choose the minimal Lexical node and plugin set that maps cleanly to markdown output. Avoid enabling rich-text features that cannot be written back to .md files without lossy conversion.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-04 Implement file-save API endpoint
items.body: Add a write endpoint such as PUT /api/files/*path with path validation, normalized error responses, and safe handling for missing files and write failures.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-06 Build editor shell and read/edit mode toggle
items.body: Add a Lexical editor mode within the existing document route so users can switch between read and edit flows without losing navigation context.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-07 Implement markdown import/export with Lexical
items.body: Load raw .md into Lexical on open and export edited content back to markdown on save. Verify stable round-tripping for the supported feature set.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-08 Preserve unsupported blocks safely
items.body: Handle Mermaid fences, unknown HTML, or unsupported markdown constructs without destructive edits. Prefer raw-block preservation, read-only rendering, or explicit fallback behavior.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-09 Add image insertion and disk-backed asset flow
items.body: Support inserting images by copying or uploading files into a project-relative asset location and serializing them to relative markdown image paths.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-10 Add editor UX for save, reload, and unsaved changes
items.body: Add save actions, keyboard shortcuts, feedback for save success or failure, and protection against losing unsaved changes during navigation or reload.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-11 Add automated coverage for edit/save round-trips
items.body: Add tests for markdown import/export fidelity, save endpoint behavior, and key document-editing flows including image serialization.

items.title: [LEXICAL] LEX-12 Update docs and rollout constraints
items.body: Document the Lexical editor architecture, markdown feature limits, image asset conventions, and local-file save behavior in README and project notes.

items.title: Migrate markdown workspace to source-first editor with external comments and section index
items.body:
Goal: Replace the current Lexical-based markdown workflow with a GitHub-style source-first experience.

Tags: editor, markdown, codemirror, preview, comments, anchors, outline, migration

Scope:
- Raw markdown remains the only source of truth
- Replace Lexical edit mode with CodeMirror 6
- Replace Lexical viewer with react-markdown preview
- Keep comments external to markdown files
- Add robust hybrid comment anchors
- Add heading/section summary index from markdown AST

Non-goals:
- LLM-generated summaries
- Rich-text round-trip editing
- atjson-based document storage

Verification:
- Saving preserves markdown exactly as typed in source mode
- Preview renders supported markdown consistently
- Comments persist outside markdown files and survive normal edits
- Section index links align with rendered headings
- Existing load/save flows continue to work

items.title: Replace Lexical viewer with react-markdown preview pipeline
items.body:
Tags: preview, markdown, react-markdown, rendering

Scope:
- Replace Lexical viewer with markdown preview rendering
- Use react-markdown with remark-gfm, rehype-highlight, and rehype-slug
- Keep heading ids stable for anchor navigation and comments
- Support existing code block rendering and markdown images

Sub-tasks:
- Build MarkdownPreview component
- Configure remark/rehype pipeline
- Add safe handling for raw HTML decision if needed
- Ensure preview layout works on desktop and mobile
- Align preview theme with existing app styles

Verification:
- GFM lists, tables, task lists, links, headings, blockquotes, and code blocks render correctly
- Heading anchors are deterministic
- Preview does not mutate saved content
- Build and typecheck pass

items.title: Replace Lexical editor with CodeMirror 6 source editor
items.body:
Tags: editor, codemirror, markdown, source-mode

Scope:
- Introduce CodeMirror 6 editor component for raw markdown
- Use @uiw/react-codemirror and @codemirror/lang-markdown
- Preserve existing dirty state, save shortcut, save/cancel behavior, and navigation blocking
- Keep markdown string as the only editable state

Sub-tasks:
- Add CodeMirror dependencies
- Build MarkdownSourceEditor component
- Wire route state from Lexical editor to raw source editor
- Preserve theme styling and mobile usability
- Remove Lexical-only edit restrictions and notices that no longer apply

Verification:
- Open file, edit markdown, save, reload, and confirm text matches exactly
- Cmd/Ctrl+S saves correctly
- Cancel restores server version
- Navigation blocker still protects unsaved changes

items.title: Implement external comment workflow for preview selections
items.body:
Tags: comments, annotations, preview, sidebar, api

Scope:
- Keep comments outside markdown files
- Reuse existing comment storage and sidebar concepts
- Allow users to select preview text and create anchored comments
- Highlight matching comment ranges in rendered preview

Sub-tasks:
- Mount AnnotationStoreProvider in the markdown route
- Integrate comment sidebar into page layout
- Capture preview text selection for comment creation
- Persist comments through the external API/store only
- Highlight and select active comment anchors in preview

Verification:
- Creating a comment does not change markdown file content
- New comments appear in sidebar and highlight the selected text
- Reload restores comments from the external store
- Deleting or resolving comments updates UI correctly

items.title: Build markdown section parser and summary index sidebar
items.body:
Tags: outline, summary, headings, ast, navigation

Scope:
- Extract headings and section ranges directly from markdown AST
- Build a navigable summary/index similar to a document outline
- Use structural excerpts only, not LLM summaries

Libraries:
- unified
- remark-parse
- remark-gfm
- unist-util-visit
- mdast-util-to-string
- github-slugger

Section model:
- slug
- depth
- title
- startOffset
- endOffset
- excerpt
- children

Sub-tasks:
- Build markdown section extraction utility
- Generate stable heading slugs shared with preview
- Render nested summary/index sidebar
- Scroll preview to selected section
- Optionally sync source editor selection later

Verification:
- Heading tree matches the markdown structure
- Clicking an index item scrolls to the correct heading
- Excerpts come from the correct section body
- Repeated heading names still get stable unique slugs

items.title: Upgrade comment anchoring to hybrid source-aware recovery
items.body:
Tags: comments, anchors, resilience, recovery, source-text

Scope:
- Move from quote-only anchors to a hybrid anchor snapshot inspired by with-md
- Use dom-anchor-text-quote for selection capture and dom-anchor-text-position as an additional hint
- Store enough metadata to recover anchors after normal markdown edits

Anchor fields:
- textQuote
- prefix
- suffix
- rangeStart
- rangeEnd
- headingPath
- fallbackLine

Sub-tasks:
- Expand comment schema and types for new anchor fields
- Capture heading path and fallback line during comment creation
- Implement ordered recovery resolver for source text and preview
- Handle duplicate quote disambiguation with context scoring
- Mark comments detached when recovery fails instead of guessing silently

Verification:
- Comments survive small edits before and after the quoted text
- Duplicate text passages resolve to the correct section using context
- Comments on changed text become detached instead of attaching to the wrong content
- Existing comments migrate or fail safely

items.title: Add migration dependencies and shared markdown utilities
items.body:
Tags: dependencies, utilities, setup

Scope:
- Add CodeMirror and markdown preview dependencies
- Add mdast section parsing dependencies
- Consolidate shared markdown helpers used by preview, comments, and index

Sub-tasks:
- Install and lock dependency versions
- Create shared slug/heading utilities
- Create shared markdown parsing helpers
- Remove unused Lexical-specific dependencies only after migration completes

Verification:
- Dependency install is reproducible
- Type imports resolve cleanly
- No duplicate slug logic exists in multiple places

items.title: Retire Lexical-specific code paths after migration
items.body:
Tags: cleanup, lexical, migration, debt

Scope:
- Remove obsolete Lexical editor/viewer code once replacements are live
- Delete markdown editability warnings that only existed for Lexical round-tripping
- Keep only code needed for source-first markdown editing

Sub-tasks:
- Remove unused components and helpers
- Remove dead imports and route branches
- Reassess package.json for removable Lexical packages
- Update any tests or docs that reference Lexical

Verification:
- No route imports Lexical components anymore
- Typecheck and build pass after cleanup
- No dead code remains for the old editor path

items.title: Run migration verification across editing, comments, and summary flows
items.body:
Tags: verification, qa, regression, build

Scope:
- Verify the full source-first workflow end to end
- Cover save fidelity, comment persistence, and summary/index behavior

Test matrix:
- Plain markdown file with headings and code blocks
- GFM file with tables and task lists
- File with repeated phrases for anchor disambiguation
- File with repeated heading names for slug uniqueness
- Mobile and desktop layouts

Verification:
- npm run typecheck passes
- npm run build passes
- Saving preserves exact markdown text
- Comments survive reload and routine edits without touching file content
- Summary index navigates correctly in preview

items.title: Polish markdown view page layout and post-save behavior
items.body:
Goal: Refine the markdown view page layout and immediate save feedback.

Tags: ui, layout, preview, outline, save-flow

Scope:
- Replace the sticky top action bar behavior with a static header layout
- Keep the document content below the header in its own scrollable view area
- Simplify the Summary panel to show headings only
- Keep the Summary panel on the right side
- Show the latest raw markdown content immediately after save in source mode flow

Verification:
- Header stays fixed in normal document flow and does not overlay content
- Main content area scrolls cleanly below the header
- Summary shows heading titles only with correct navigation
- Summary remains on the right side on supported layouts
- After save, the visible source content reflects the saved markdown immediately without stale state

items.title: Make markdown view header static with content scrolling below
items.body:
Tags: ui, layout, header, scrolling

Scope:
- Replace the sticky top bar on the markdown view page with a static header
- Keep actions and breadcrumbs visible in the page header area
- Move document scrolling into the content region below the header

Sub-tasks:
- Audit current view-page layout containers and sticky behavior
- Remove sticky positioning from the top bar
- Ensure preview/editor region scrolls independently below the header
- Preserve responsive layout on desktop and mobile

Verification:
- Header no longer sticks while scrolling document content
- Content scroll starts below the header without overlap
- Edit/view actions remain accessible and correctly positioned

items.title: Simplify Summary panel to headings only on the left side
items.body:
Tags: ui, summary, outline, navigation

Scope:
- Remove excerpt text from the Summary panel
- Keep only heading labels in the outline
- Move the Summary panel to the left side

Sub-tasks:
- Update Summary item rendering to heading-only rows
- Keep heading nesting/indentation behavior
- Move the Summary panel to the left side in view and edit modes
- Ensure small-screen layout still behaves sensibly

Verification:
- Summary renders heading titles only
- Clicking a heading still navigates to the correct section
- Summary remains on the left side for wide layouts

comments.author: OpenCode
comments.body: Ticket updated to reflect the current requirement: Summary belongs on the left side, not the right.

comments.author: OpenCode
comments.body: Implemented the updated requirement: Summary now renders on the left side on wide layouts and remains headings-only. The markdown content, Summary, and comments panels now each have their own scroll container under the app header. Verified with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build`.

items.title: Render direct markdown file routes inside the app shell
items.body:
Tags: bug, routing, markdown, app-shell, rendering

Scope:
- Direct markdown file routes such as `/AGENTS.md` currently show raw markdown content by itself
- These routes should instead render within the normal application shell/layout
- The markdown content should remain readable, but it must stay inside the app experience with navigation and surrounding UI

Sub-tasks:
- Identify why direct markdown file URLs bypass the app-shell experience
- Route raw markdown file requests back through the normal document page
- Ensure markdown content renders inside the app layout instead of standalone raw output
- Verify normal in-app document viewing still behaves consistently

Verification:
- Visiting `/AGENTS.md` shows the document inside the app shell
- Navigation, sidebars, and page layout are present
- Markdown browsing through the app continues to work as before

comments.author: OpenCode
comments.body: Fixed as a bug in `app/routes/$.tsx`. Save now keeps the user in source mode and updates `draft` from the saved server response immediately, so the latest raw markdown stays visible after save. Verified with `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build`.

comments.author: OpenCode
comments.body: Ticket corrected: the real issue is that direct markdown file routes like `/AGENTS.md` should render raw markdown without the app chrome. The earlier change in `app/routes/$.tsx` fixed a separate save-flow UX issue but did not address this raw-route bug.

comments.author: OpenCode
comments.body: Ticket corrected again: the actual bug is the opposite of the prior rewrite. Direct markdown routes like `/AGENTS.md` currently show raw content by itself, but they should render inside the normal app shell.

comments.author: OpenCode
comments.body: Fixed the actual routing bug in `scripts/dev-server.js` and `scripts/markdown-viewer.js`. Direct `.md` requests like `/AGENTS.md` are now sent through the SPA entrypoint before Vite/static file serving can return the raw file. Verified with live HTTP checks against both dev and production servers, plus `npm run typecheck` and `npm run build`.
